behaviors.description: Renamed files appear in their own category and are not also counted as a deletion plus an addition.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: the failure is primarily classification/counting logic (rename should map to only one bucket), which can fail inside a local parser/aggregator function. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: no OS/network/subprocess behavior is required to trigger the core double-count bug if rename-status input is provided as fixture data. INTEGRATION-BRAKE check: (a) no required real infra, (b) no AI output, (c) no multi-agent steps, (d) no LLM-stage handoff. REJECTION-GATE: Integration/System were considered but rejected because behavior text focuses on category accounting semantics, not subprocess/runtime behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Unit-test the status classification and aggregation function with fixtures including rename statuses (for example `R100 old.py new.py`) and assert renamed_count increments while added_count/deleted_count do not.

behaviors.description: Diffs that include binary files do not cause the tool to crash; binary files are counted in the output.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: this behavior depends on real git diff/subprocess output for binary content markers and exit behavior. MOCK-HIDE: mocked text fixtures can miss real decoding/format edge cases seen from actual git on binary files. INTEGRATION-BRAKE check supports upgrade: (a) yes, real subprocess/filesystem interaction is part of the failure boundary; (b) no AI dependence; (c) no multi-agent chain. REJECTION-GATE: Integration was rejected because behavior text explicitly references real binary diffs causing runtime crash, which is an infra-boundary risk.
behaviors.test_description: Create a temp git repo, commit a binary file change, run the CLI as a real subprocess, and assert process exits successfully and report includes binary file in the appropriate count.

behaviors.description: Running the tool on a repository that has no commits yet produces an empty report rather than an error.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: unborn-branch/no-commit behavior is determined by real git command responses and exit codes. MOCK-HIDE: stubbing git success responses can hide real non-zero/edge semantics in empty repos. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) yes real subprocess/git state needed, (b) no AI output, (c) no multi-step agent pipeline. REJECTION-GATE: Integration rejected because behavior text targets actual repository state with no commits, a real git boundary condition.
behaviors.test_description: Initialize a fresh temp repo with no commits, invoke CLI subprocess, assert zero exit code and structured empty report on stdout (not an exception/error message).

behaviors.description: The counts in the report match the actual change history of the repository being analyzed.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: correctness is defined against real repository history semantics (adds/deletes/renames/modifies across commits) produced by git. MOCK-HIDE: mocked history can align with implementation assumptions and miss real git edge cases. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) yes, real subprocess/repo history is required; (b) no AI; (c) no agentic sequence. REJECTION-GATE: Integration rejected because behavior text anchors truth to the actual repository history, not just module handoff.
behaviors.test_description: Construct a temp repo with a known sequence of commits containing add/delete/rename/modify operations, run CLI subprocess, and assert reported totals exactly match the known ground truth from that history.

behaviors.description: The output is identical whether the changes being analyzed are staged or unstaged.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: staged vs unstaged is git index/worktree state, which is an OS/filesystem + git subprocess boundary. MOCK-HIDE: fakes often collapse index/worktree differences and can miss real behavior drift. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) yes real git state model needed, (b) no AI output dependency, (c) no multi-agent steps. REJECTION-GATE: Integration rejected because behavior text explicitly compares two real git states (staged and unstaged) that are infra-level semantics.
behaviors.test_description: In a temp repo, create equivalent content changes once staged and once unstaged, run CLI subprocess in both states, and assert stdout structured report is byte-identical.
